title: Chunking, Segmentation, Document Structure, and Identity · Behavior Case: Rag Chunk Unit Boundaries
summary: A pipeline splits every 1,000 characters with 500-character overlap, cuts tables and procedures in half, assigns random IDs, and assumes more overlap always increases recall. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the chunk unit boundaries increment in structure-aware chunking experiment. Learners progress through…
objectives: Compare token, sentence, paragraph, section, semantic, table, code, and layout boundaries against the target retrieval unit.
Maintain mappings from normalized tokens and chunks back to exact document spans, pages, regions, and versions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Rag Chunk Unit Boundaries, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to structure-aware chunking experiment.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-chunking-structure-5
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-chunking-structure-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer.
def worked_rag_chunking_structure_5(token_count: int = 920, chunk_size: int = 240, overlap: int = 40, boundary_count: int = 5, parent_linked: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "query5dekrow",
        "tenant9036",
        "indexworked59036",
        "prompt5dekrow9036",
        "evaluation9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if token_count <= 0 or chunk_size <= 0:
        return False, 0, "chunk-input"
    if overlap < 0 or overlap >= chunk_size:
        return False, overlap, "overlap-budget"
    if boundary_count < 1 or not parent_linked:
        return False, boundary_count, "document-structure"
    stride = chunk_size - overlap
    chunks = 1 + max(0, (token_count - chunk_size + stride - 1) // stride)
    assert chunks >= 1
    return True, chunks, "structure-aware-chunks"
